Are there any Hamilton fans on your team? The musical — not the Tiger-Cats. HEAD TO THE SQUARE NAMED FOR AMERICA'S FAVORITE FIGHTING FRENCHMAN. Google it if you must. Hurry — watch for cars and streetcars. TELL ME THE FRENCHMAN'S NAME ONCE YOU ARRIVE.
We lied.
This IS a team building exercise, but it's NOT a walking tour. Think Escape Room meets Scavenger Hunt. Type PLAY to find out what team you're on.

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: Figure out which team you're on using the Team Cipher puzzle.<button class="open-cipher-btn" onclick="openTeamOverlay()">Open Team Cipher →</button>

incorrect.html: Hmm, check that spelling! If you're stuck, text 504-581-5652 for help from Mission Control.

messages.html:
Ok, you're TEAM {{TEAM_NAME}}, but what does {{teamName}} mean?

A)  A Native American tribe
B)  A deep-fried French doughnut
C)  A little something extra
D)  A Cajun werewolf

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: {{correctMsg}}

Are there any Hamilton fans on your team? The musical — not the Tiger-Cats. HEAD TO THE SQUARE NAMED FOR AMERICA'S FAVORITE FIGHTING FRENCHMAN. Google it if you must. Hurry — watch for cars and streetcars. TELL ME THE FRENCHMAN'S NAME ONCE YOU ARRIVE.

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: Welcome to Lafayette Square, Team {{teamName}}! I'm so glad you didn't go all the way to Jackson Square. FIND THIS EXACT SPOT and then we'll take a look around. Reply with HERE! when you get to the exact spot.

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: 📸 Designate a team photographer. Take a team photo here. To win this game you not only need to be the first to finish, you also need to show all of your pics! Type CLICK to continue.
You're standing in the former epicenter of New Orleans politics.
Henry Clay is here in the middle of Lafayette Square for no apparent reason. He founded the Whig Party that, upon its collapse, made way for the Republican party.
Straight across the street, on St. Charles Avenue (where the streetcar runs) is Gallier Hall, that used to be our City Hall.
To the right of Gallier Hall is a boring looking building that Henry Clay is kind of gesturing towards, and it may secretly be the most fascinating of all.

Opposite St. Charles Avenue, on Camp Street, there's a huge building to your right on the other side of the square that serves as the U.S. Court of Appeals building, but it started as a good ol' post office. Can you see the statues on top?
Enough sightseeing. I was stalling. Hopefully Team {{partnerName}} has arrived. We need their help.
Stay where you are. Call out HEY TEAM {{PARTNER_NAME}}. YOUR PASSWORD IS {{give}}. WHAT IS OUR PASSWORD?

What password did Team {{PARTNER_NAME}} give you?

A)  Etouffee
B)  Gumbo
C)  Jambalaya
D)  Muffuletta

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: Correct! {{receiveBlurb}}
Next, let's focus back on Gallier Hall. A president's funeral was held here — just not a United States president. The Confederate States of America had a president too. What was his last name?

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html:
Jefferson Davis was President of the CONFEDERATE States of America — not the United States! 😮 Here's a picture from his funeral, right at Gallier Hall, 135 years ago: https://cdm16880.contentdm.oclc.org/digital/collection/p16880coll18

Now send me a photo of you or your team with Henry Clay in the background. Why he's hanging out in Lafayette Square, I'll never know.
Correct!

Now. Look up! Atop the roof of the John Minor Wisdom U.S. Court of Appeals Building — the one across from you on Camp Street — are four sets of bronze sculptures known as 'The Ladies': Agriculture, History, Industry and Arts. Actually there are 16 ladies because there are 4 identical statues on the roof. One on each corner. They were sculpted by Daniel Chester French.
📸 Take a team photo of your team looking up at The Ladies.

Daniel Chester French also designed another famous U.S. memorial. Do you know which one?

A)  The Lincoln Memorial
B)  The Statue of Liberty
C)  The Washington Monument
D)  Mount Rushmore

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: Oh yeah! 🎉

Next, take a look at the plain building to the right of Gallier Hall — that's the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta's New Orleans branch.

TELL ME HOW MANY DOLLARS A DAY they're talking about in the video below. Watch at least the first minute now to get the answer and you can watch the whole video later.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mwCfK1oqRkI

A)  $42.50
B)  1 MILLION DOLLARS
C)  6 MILLION DOLLARS
D)  1 TRILLION DOLLARS

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: Let's wander around the square for a minute. FIND A TREE PLANTED BY A BUSH AND TELL ME WHAT TYPE OF TREE IT IS.

A)  Bald Cypress
B)  Crepe Myrtle
C)  Shumard Oak
D)  Weeping Willow

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

stops.name: A tree planted by a Bush

messages.html: The original monument got replaced because of a misspelled word and the original tree got replaced because it died, but hey, it's the thought that counts!

There's a sculpture across Camp Street — kinda looks like a pointing arrow. TAKE A SHORT VIDEO OF YOUR TEAM ROTATING THE SCULPTURE AND HAVE IT POINT TO THE NAME OF A FOUNDING FATHER. TELL ME HIS LAST NAME.

💡 HINT: Do NOT text me "Benjamin Franklin". You may have to strain your neck a bit to find his name written in stone. He was the first Chief Justice of the United States, appointed by George Washington himself. Try not to Google it.

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html:
There's a small monument on Poydras Street with a big story. In 1850, Venezuelan-born general Narciso López — who had designed what would become his adopted country's flag — sailed from New Orleans with hundreds of soldiers, carrying that flag there for the very first time in history.

Find the monument: https://what3words.com/remake.king.cloth

Oh shoot, that map tells you the name of the country. I tell you what...

Type CLICK to continue.

📸 Take a team photo at the monument. Save it for later.

Type CLICK to continue.

What do Lee Harvey Oswald, the CIA, FBI and Secret Service all have in common? They all had offices in this neighborhood at one time or another.

📸 Take a team photo in front of the building. Save it for later.

He investigated a murder. He questioned the answer. He boarded a plane and vanished. The building next to you bears his name. Who was he?

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: TAKE A PIC OF THE BEACON OF LIGHT STANDING BETWEEN JULIA AND HER BROTHER GIROD. Send me the name of the restaurant we're going to.

incorrect.html: Ooh, not quite! 🤔 Text 504-581-5652 and Mission Control will get you sorted.

messages.html: 🎉 You've completed the game! But wait! Quick! 🏃 Text {{TEAM_NAME}} to 504.581.5652 right now (don't type it here). The first team to do so wins a prize! 🏆
